  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 100,

  "sound_source": "water hammer",
  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate piping support or missing water hammer arrestors."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install an ASSE 1010 certified water hammer arrestor close to the fast-closing valve.",
    "Secure loose PEX and copper piping with approved hangers and straps per code spacing."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording clearly captures a fast-closing valve followed by a loud, metallic banging sound characteristic of water hammer. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, and violates standard plumbing installation practices.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer)",
    "ASSE 1010 (Water Hammer Arrestors)"
  ]
}
